info.description: The purpose of the Card Management System APIs is to manage the card agreements (also called card contracts) and the cards
info.title: APIs exposed by the Card Management System

servers.description: Generated server url. When deploying this API it is important to keep the basePath as 'issuing' like in the example above.

post.summary: Create a card agreement and dependencies (subscription) (CMS4_001)
post.description:
In Estreem's dictionnary, the term 'agreement' is used to represent the 'contract'.
Within the subscription service, the bank provides a cardholder identifier and may provide an owner identifier and/or a branch identifier. The cardholder, owner and branch data will be retrieved from the bank using these identifiers and through services exposed by the bank.
The subscription service covers the creation of both physical and digital cards. The product code and the type of card support allows to identify if a card is created under a physical or digital form.
After subscription, depending of the card product configuration, the cardStatus can be either 'validated' or 'activated'.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

get.summary: Search for the data of all card agreements of a cardholder (CMS27_001)
get.description:
Search for the data of all card agreements of a cardholder
Common parameters used for card manufacturing and associated to the card agreement are provided in the card objet in the response. The card object doesn't represent real card.

Request to retrieve card data associated to the card.

Afterwards, it is used to access the card ressource on other APIs.

get.description:
Request to retrieve the secret code associated to the card (PIN)

The public part of the RSA key is required in the header request and it will be used to encrypt the card data in the response.

Afterwards, it is used to access the card ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

x-enumDescriptions.OBNK: Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)

x-enumDescriptions.USR2: User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)

get.summary: Search for the data of all cards of a card agreement (CMS1_004)
get.description:
The card selection code allows to restrict the cards selected to get the data.
If the card selection code is absent, all cards are selected.
If the card selection code is present, the cards are selected based on their status with the following options : only activated card, only card waiting to be activated, cards wich are active and waiting to be activated, only blocked card, only locked card, the last card created and card waiting to be activated.

Elements shared by all cards of a card agreement (eg. options or usage limits) are provided in separated lists in the response.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.description:
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: cardSelectionCode

schema.description:
Card filter selection code
Code to restrict the cards selected to get the details
If absent all cards are selected
If present, the cards are selected based on their status as per the following values
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ACTV : Only activated card
TOAC : Only card waiting to be activated
AATA : Cards wich are active and waiting to be activated
BLKD : Only blocked card
LCKD : Only locked card
LATA : The last card created and card waiting to be activated

post.summary: Initiate individual card agreement surveillance activation (CMS11_001)
post.description:
By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual card agreement surveillance deactivation (CMS11_001a)
post.description:
The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual card unlocking (CMS11_002a)
post.description:
The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

put.summary: Initiate usage limit(s) creation or modification for a card agreement  (CMS11_003)
put.description:
1. For each usage limit :
The type of value for the usage limit (usageLimitValueType) is mandatory and defines whether the usage limit is an amount or a number.
The definition of the 'number' type is reserved for future use.
If the type is 'amount' then the personalized amount must be provided. The currency of the amount is defined in the bank configuration of the CMS.
If no start date/time and no end date/time are provided in the request, the action is a modification of a permanent usage limit.
If only one date/time (the start date/time or the end date/time) is provided, an error is generated.
Both start date/time and end date/time must be provided in the request to make an action on a temporary usage limit :
- if no temporary usage limit is defined for the usage limit code, a temporary usage limit is created
- if a temporary usage limit is already defined for the usage limit code, the temporary usage limit is modified.

2. Only one category (permanent limits or temporary limits) can be set for one call to this service :
- If all limits are correctly created or modified, the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used in all cases whether the usage limits are created or modified.
- If one limit cannot be created or modified, a 400 error is returned and no limit is created or modified
- If permanent limits and temporary limits are set in the request, a 400 error with code SB_DC_MAP0000151 is returned and no limit is created or modified

delete.summary: Initiate individual temporary usage limit deletion for a card agreement (CMS11_012)
delete.description: Only a temporary usage limit can be deleted

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: code

schema.description:
Usage limit code
Code defining the context of use of the usage limit
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PDOM : Paiement domestique (Purchase DOMestic)
PINT : Paiement international (Purchase INTernational)​
PTOT : Paiement total (Purchase TOTal)
WGRD : Retrait domestique groupe (Withdrawal GRoup Domestic)
WNGD : Retrait domestique hors groupe et cash manuel domestique (Withdrawal Not Group Domestic)​
WGRI : Retrait international groupe (Withdrawal GRoup International)​
WNGI : Retrait international hors groupe et cash manuel international (Withdrawal Not Group International)
WTOT : Retrait total (Withdrawal TOTal)​

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

parameters.name: usageRestrictionCode

schema.description:
Card usage restriction code
Code defining the usage restriction of the card
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
MOTO : Restriction on MOTO transactions
WDRL : Restriction on withdrawal transactions
PRCH : Restriction on purchase transactions
CTLS : Restriction on contactless transactions
GEOB : Restriction on geoblocking : the zone/region for which the geoblocking is applied must be provided in the usageRestrictionData

post.description: Initiate individual usage restriction deletion of a card agreement

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: usageRestrictionCode

schema.description:
Card usage restriction code
Code defining the usage restriction of the card
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
MOTO : Restriction on MOTO transactions
WDRL : Restriction on withdrawal transactions
PRCH : Restriction on purchase transactions
CTLS : Restriction on contactless transactions
GEOB : Restriction on geoblocking : the zone/region for which the geoblocking is applied must be provided in the usageRestrictionData

parameters.description:

This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)

OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown

USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual contactless locking (CMS11_005)
post.description:
The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

post.summary: Initiate individual contactless unlocking (CMS11_005a)
post.description:
The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card identifier
Unique identifier of the card.
This identifier is generated by the CMS during the subscription, during a remanufacturing or during an opposition if a card is remanufactured.
Afterwards, it is used to access the card ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

put.summary: Initiate individual option creation or update of a card agreement (CMS11_006)
put.description: Initiate individual option creation or update of a card agreement

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.description:
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: optionCode

schema.description:
Card agreement option code
Code defining the option associated to the card agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
Example of values and label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
TRAVEL_OPTION : Travel option (subscription for reduced or canceled fees when using card in foreign countries)
CASH_LIMIT : Limit for cash that can be provided to a cardholder (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData
DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT : Limit for delegated withdrawal (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData

delete.summary: Initiate individual option deletion of a card agreement (CMS11_006a)
delete.description: Initiate individual option deletion of a card agreement

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.description:
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: optionCode

schema.description:
Card agreement option code
Code defining the option associated to the card agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
Example of values and label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
TRAVEL_OPTION : Travel option (subscription for reduced or canceled fees when using card in foreign countries)
CASH_LIMIT : Limit for cash that can be provided to a cardholder (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData
DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT : Limit for delegated withdrawal (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual confidential code (PIN) reprinting for a card agreement (CMS11_007)
post.description:
The request to reprint the confidential code is transmitted to the manufacturer in charge of printing the mailer.
The manufacturer will send the mailer to the cardholder via postal services.

post.summary: Initiate individual card blocking (CMS11_008)
post.description:
By default, the blocking service of the CMS initiates the manufacturing of a new card when blocking the current card.
A remanufacturing indicator allows to explicitly define the expected behavior to remanufacture a new card or not.
For banks calculating the PAN on their system and which need to provide the PAN to the CMS, this remanufacturing indicator must be set to false and the bank must use the remanufacturing service to initiate the creation of a new card in order to be able to provide the new PAN to the CMS.

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual card activation (CMS11_009)
post.description:
The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

Request to deactivate the neutralisation phase associated to the card security code (CVx2) and managed by the IAS system.
This neutralisation mecanism is described on the IAS documentation.

The body of the request is empty

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card identifier
Unique identifier of the card.
This identifier is generated by the CMS during the subscription, during a remanufacturing or during an opposition if a card is remanufactured.
Afterwards, it is used to access the card ressource on other APIs.

schema.description: This idempotency key is used on APIs which are, by nature, not idempotent.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

x-enumDescriptions.USR2: User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)

post.summary: Initiate individual activation of the cardholder consent (CMS11_011)
post.description:
The cardholder has given its consent to publish its card agreement and card data to the card networks.
These data will be published to the card networks automatically upon renewal of the card.

The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 204 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual deactivation of the cardholder consent (CMS11_011a)

These data won't be published to the card networks automatically upon renewal of the card.

The body of the request is empty

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

put.summary: Initiate individual update of the entity linked to the card agreement (CMS11_015)
put.description:
Update of the identifier of the entity linked to the card agreement

The body of the request is empty

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.description:
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: entityId

schema.description:
Card agreement entity Id
Identifier of the physical or logical entity linked to the card contract/agreement (entity that signed the contract with the cardholder; for example, a branch).
This identifier is provided to the CMS during the subscription by the bank.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the entity data from the bank and by the IAS in messages sent to the bank

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate individual card agreement termination (CMS4_002)
post.description:
If the date/time is present in the request, the card agreement will be terminated on that specific date and time
Otherwise (no date/time present), the card agreement will be terminated on the expiration of the current active card (as a consequence stopping the automatic renewal process).
After the card agreement termination, the cardAgreementStatus is set to closed and all active cards are deactivated (their cardStatus is set to deactivated)

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).
Thus, instead of using the 201 'created' HTTP code (usually reserved to the creation of a new resource on a classic POST), the 200 'OK' HTTP code is used to remind that there is no resource creation but an action done on an existing resource.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Initiate an individual card remanufacturing (CMS4_003)
post.description:
After remanufacturing, depending of the card product configuration, the cardStatus can be either 'validated' or 'activated'.

By convention, on a POST, if the term 'action' placed before a conjugated verb ('ing' form) is present in the URI, it designates an action realised on an existing resource (ex. : locking, unlocking, canceling, activating, deactivating, ...) but not the creation of a new resource as the definition of the POST method would have it (this is an exception case).

put.summary: Initiate individual update of the card agreement account (CMS5_001)
put.description:
Update of the identifier of the banking account associated to the card agreement

The body of the request is empty

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

parameters.name: accountId

schema.description:
Account Id linked to the card
Technical identifier of the banking account associated to the card contract/agreement
This identifier is provided during the subscription by the bank.
It will be sent by the CMS to the IAS so that the IAS can use this identifier during the authorisation process to contact the banking Host (for balance check) or for standin purposes.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

put.summary: Initiate shipping condition creation or modification of a card agreement (CMS5_002)
put.description:
Creation or modification of one or multiple shipping condition

At least one shipping condition must be created or modified.
A permanent shipping condition must be modified using:
- a shipping condition code
- an entity identifier or a postal address identifier (one identifier is required)
A temporary shipping condition must be created or modified using:
- a shipping condition code
- a start date/time and an end date/time (both date/time are required for a temporary shipping condition)
- an entity identifier or a postal address identifier (one identifier is required)
A complete postal address can be provided only for a temporary shipping condition (this complete postal addresse is optional).
A new start date/time or a new of end date/time provided in the request will update the temporary shipping condition.
It is not possible to update permanent and temporary conditions at the same time.

Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

post.summary: Information of updates to cardholder data (CMS5_003)
post.description:
Request to provide the indication that data of a cardholder have been updated in the banking system.
The cardhodler data are provided in the request.
The service is intended for banks which expose a service to retrieve cardholder data by the CMS so that the banks can provide new data between retrievals initiated by the CMS.
This service should be used when the bank is using the CMS services to send data to other parties related to card management (for example, operations management, concierge services) so that the CMS can forward these updates to these parties.
If the bank doesn't need the CMS to send data to other parties, this service is not required to be used as the CMS will retrieve data from the bank when needed.
Note : The data are not stored by the CMS.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Cardholder Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder in the banking system.
This identifier is provided during the subscription by the bank.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the cardholder data from the bank.

parameters.name: acquiringChannel
parameters.description:
Communication channel used to initiate the request
This channel identifies the origin of the request such as a web banking application, a branch system, a back-office system, etc.
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BKOF : Back office (back-office agents)
BTCH : Batch processing (reserved value for the processor’s batch processing of files
BRNH : Branch (customer advisor)
CCNT : Call Center
OBNK :Open banking (selfcare on mobiles or tablets)
UNKN : Unknown
USR1 : User defined 1 (usage defined by the bank)
USR2 : User defined 2  (usage defined by the bank)
USIN : User interface (reserved value for the processor’s UIs)
WBNK : Web banking (selfcare on a computer)

post.summary: Information of updates to card agreement owner data (CMS5_004)
post.description:
Request to provide the indication that data of a card agreement owner have been updated in the banking system.
The owner data are provided in the request :
- For an individual owner, the following data are required ownerCivility, ownerFirstName and ownerLastName
- For a business owner, the ownerCompanyName is required
The service is intended for banks which expose a service to retrieve owner data by the CMS so that the banks can provide new data between retrievals initiated by the CMS.
This service should be used when the bank is using the CMS services to send data to other parties related to card management (for example, operations management, concierge services) so that the CMS can forward these updates to these parties.
If the bank doesn't need the CMS to send data to other parties, this service is not required to be used as the CMS will retrieve data from the bank when needed.
Note : The data are not stored by the CMS.

parameters.name: id

schema.description:
Card agreement owner id
Technical identifier of the owner of the card agreement in the banking system.
This identifier can be provided during the subscription by the bank if the owner of the card agreement is different from the cardholder.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the owner data from the bank.

parameters.description:
Technical identifier of the owner of the card agreement in the banking system.

tags.name: Card Agreements
tags.description: Card agreements management (including the card creation service)

tags.name: Card Holders
tags.description: Card holders data updates provided by the bank

tags.name: Card Agreement Owners
tags.description: Card agreement owners data updates provided by the bank

bearer_auth.description: This security scheme uses Bearer authentication with JWT tokens. The authentication token is provided by Estreem’s IAM service during authentication process of the calling system. The calling system must send to each service the authentication token provided by Estreem’s IAM in order to use an Estreem service. To authenticate, include the token in the `Authorization` header of your HTTP request: `Authorization: Bearer `. You can enter your token using the "Authorize" button in Swagger UI.

moreInfo.description: Link to more info about the error

id.description:
Cardholder Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder in the banking system.
This identifier is provided during the subscription by the bank.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the cardholder data from the bank.

Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

entityId.description:
Card agreement entity Id
Identifier of the physical or logical entity linked to the card contract/agreement (entity that signed the contract with the cardholder; for example, a branch).
This identifier is provided to the CMS during the subscription by the bank.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the entity data from the bank and by the IAS in messages sent to the bank

accountId.description:
Account Id linked to the card
Technical identifier of the banking account associated to the card contract/agreement
This identifier is provided during the subscription by the bank.
It will be sent by the CMS to the IAS so that the IAS can use this identifier during the authorisation process to contact the banking Host (for balance check) or for standin purposes.

id.description:
Card agreement owner id
Technical identifier of the owner of the card agreement in the banking system.
This identifier can be provided during the subscription by the bank if the owner of the card agreement is different from the cardholder.
Afterwards, it is used by the CMS to retrieve the owner data from the bank.

additionalInformationZone.description:
Card agreement additional information zone
This data allows to send discretionnary data from the bank to the card manufacturer or, in return, from the card manufacturer to the bank.
The CMS does not perform any action on this data.

chainingId.description:
Card agreement identifier of the parent card
Technical identifier of the card agreement from which data will be reused upon renewing the agreement (for example, retrieving the PIN code or personalized limits)

deferredCalendarCode.description:
Deferred calendar code
Code defining the deferred calendar to be used in case of a deferred debit card
Example of values
---------------------------------------------------------------------
LAST_DAY_ON_MONTH
FIRST_DAY_ON_MONTH
MIDDLE_DAY_ON_MONTH
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.

code.description:
Card agreement option code
Code defining the option associated to the card agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
Example of values and label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
TRAVEL_OPTION : Travel option (subscription for reduced or canceled fees when using card in foreign countries)
CASH_LIMIT : Limit for cash that can be provided to a cardholder (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData
DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT : Limit for delegated withdrawal (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData

data.description:
Data for the option
The values depend on the option code and are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
For example, the value must be present if optionCode is CASH_LIMIT or DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT

code.description:
Shipping condition code
Shipping condition code of the card contract/agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.

entityId.description:
Shipping condition entity Id
Identifier of the physical entity to be used for the shipping condition
For example, a branch to send the card

postalAddressId.description:
Shipping condition postal address identifier
The data of the postal address (this identifier and its corresponding details) will be obtained upon retrieval of the cardholder data.

code.description:
Usage limit code
Code defining the context of use of the usage limit
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PDOM : Paiement domestique (Purchase DOMestic)
PINT : Paiement international (Purchase INTernational)​
PTOT : Paiement total (Purchase TOTal)
WGRD : Retrait domestique groupe (Withdrawal GRoup Domestic)
WNGD : Retrait domestique hors groupe et cash manuel domestique (Withdrawal Not Group Domestic)​
WGRI : Retrait international groupe (Withdrawal GRoup International)​
WNGI : Retrait international hors groupe et cash manuel international (Withdrawal Not Group International)
WTOT : Retrait total (Withdrawal TOTal)​

valueType.description:
Type of value of the usage limit
Type defining whether the usage limit is an amount or a number
In the current definition, only amounts are supported.
The definition of the "number" type is reserved for future use
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
AMNT : Amount
NUBR : Number (RFU)

personalizedAmount.description:
Personalized amount of the usage limit
Amount of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this amount overrides the default amount of the limit)
This amount is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as AMNT
This amount can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified in a currency where the unit of currency is implied by the context and compliant with ISO 4217. The decimal separator is a dot.

personalizedNumber.description:
Personalized number of the usage limit
Number of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this number overrides the default number of the limit)
This number is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)
This number can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.

encryptedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
encrypted pan
The encryption is done using JSON Web Encryption standard.
Once decrypted it represents a Primary Account Number (PAN), which is a 16 to 19 numeric characters string.

sequenceNumber.description:
Card Sequence Number
Equivalent to DE23 in ISO8583 based protocols

expiryDate.description:
Expiry date
Format (ISO8601) : YYYY-MM ; Example 2024-12
Equivalent to DE14 in ISO8583 based protocols

alphaTestIndicator.description:
Alpha test indicator
Indicates if the card must be manufactured for alpha tests or not

cardholderPictureId.description:
Distribution identifier of the card design picture for the cardholder - may be an URL
Identifier of the card design picture for the cardholder provided by the bank during the subscription (for example, it may be an URL)

consentIndicator.description:
Consent Indicator
Indicator to register if the cardholder has given its consent to publish its card agreement and card data to the card networks.
By default no consent is registered and no data will be published to card networks
If set to true, data will be published to the card networks automatically upon renewal of the card.

contactlessActivationChoiceCode.description:
Contactless activation choice code
Code to activate or not the contacless function of the card depending on the cardholder choice at the subscription
Possible values are 'true' or 'false'

secretCodeChoiceIndicator.description:
PIN choice feature indicator
Indicator to request a PIN choice process activation upon card manufacturing
By default no PIN choice process is activated (the PIN is reused from a previous card or generated by the CMS)
If set to true, a PIN choice process is initiated with the cardholder

urgentManufacturingIndicator.description:
Urgent manufacturing indicator
Indicator to request an urgent manufacturing of the card
By default no urgent manufacturing is requested

designCode.description:
Card design code defined by the distribution
Code to define the design selected by the cardholder during the subscription and to be used to print the card

designSubscriptionIndicator.description:
Card design choice subscription indicator
Indicates if the card design has been chosen by the cardholder during the subscription
By default no card design is chosen

embossedComplementaryData.description:
Complementary embossed zone label
Complementary line for the embossed zone to be printed on the card

embossedZone.description:
Card embossed zone label
Label to use for the embossed zone printed on the card in case the default format needs to be overridden

paymentAccountReference.description:
Payment account reference
(PAR)
Equivalent to DE57.0056 in CBAE protocol (domestic french data)

productCode.description:
Product Code
Product code of the card contract/agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.

supplementZone1.description:
First supplementary information to be sent to the card manufacturer
First supplementary information to be sent to the card manufacturer.
This is used for printing the letter for the card

supplementZone2.description:
Second supplementary information to be sent to the card manufacturer
Second supplementary information to be sent to the card manufacturer.
This is used for printing the letter for the mailer

id.description:
Card agreement Id
Technical identifier of the cardholder card agreement.
If not provided by the bank in the cardAgreement creation, the identifier is generated by the CMS and provided in the response to the cardAgreement creation (subscription).
Afterwards, it is used to access the cardAgreement ressource on other APIs.

Card identifier
Unique identifier of the card.
This identifier is generated by the CMS during the subscription, during a remanufacturing or during an opposition if a card is remanufactured.
Afterwards, it is used to access the card ressource on other APIs.

primaryAccountNumberId.description:
Primary Account Number ID
Identifier (also called PANid) associated to the PAN of the card.
The PANid is generated (in the form of an UUID v4) by Estreem during the processing of the subscription request sent to the CMS (either when the PAN is calculated by the CMS or when the PAN is provided by the bank).
The PANid is provided to the bank in the subscription response.
The PANid is used in further exchanges of the CMS and the IAS.

encryptedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
encrypted pan
The encryption is done using JSON Web Encryption standard.
Once decrypted it represents a Primary Account Number (PAN), which is a 16 to 19 numeric characters string.

maskedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
Masked Primary Account Number
Displayable version of the PAN with masked caracters

sequenceNumber.description:
Card Sequence Number
Equivalent to DE23 in ISO8583 based protocols

expiryDate.description:
Expiry date
Format (ISO8601) : YYYY-MM ; Example 2024-12
Equivalent to DE14 in ISO8583 based protocols

paymentAccountReference.description:
Payment account reference
(PAR)
Equivalent to DE57.0056 in CBAE protocol (domestic french data)

CardStatusCodeset.description:
Card status
Status of the card indicating the capacity to use the card or not
After subscription or remanufacturing, depending of the card product configuration, the cardStatus can be either 'validated' or 'activated'.
After card activation, the cardStatus is 'activated'.
After card blocking, the cardStatus is 'blocked'.
After card agreement termination, the cardStatus is 'deactivated'.
After activation of a new card and if the old card is deactivated, the cardStatus of the old card is 'deactivated'.
Intermediate statuses 'created/declared' or 'in progress' can be visible in UIs or can be obtained on research API services only in case of errors but they are not present in events
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
CREA : Created/declared
VALD : Validated
ACIP : Activation in progress
ACTV : Activated
BLIP : Blocking in progress
BLKD : Blocked
DEIP : Deactivation in progress
DCTD : Deactivated

CardSupportTypeCodeset.description:
Card support type
Type of support for the card
The card can be physically manufactured (in this case, the card may be used digitally depending on the product configuration) or created as a digital card only
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PHSL : Physical
DGTL : Digital

primaryAccountNumberId.description:
Primary Account Number ID
Identifier (also called PANid) associated to the PAN of the card.
The PANid is generated (in the form of an UUID v4) by Estreem during the processing of the subscription request sent to the CMS (either when the PAN is calculated by the CMS or when the PAN is provided by the bank).
The PANid is provided to the bank in the subscription response.
The PANid is used in further exchanges of the CMS and the IAS.

encryptedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
encrypted pan
The encryption is done using JSON Web Encryption standard.
Once decrypted it represents a Primary Account Number (PAN), which is a 16 to 19 numeric characters string.

expiryDate.description:
Expiry date
Format (ISO8601) : YYYY-MM ; Example 2024-12
Equivalent to DE14 in ISO8583 based protocols

encryptedEphemeralSecurePinBlockKey.description: Ephemeral Symetric Secure PinBlock Key encrypted by ephemeral Transport Key

encryptedEphemeralTransportKey.description: Ephemeral Symetric Transport Key encrypted by a public RSA key

x-enumDescriptions.ISO0: PIN diversified with the card account number, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
x-enumDescriptions.ISO1: PIN completed with random padding characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
x-enumDescriptions.ISO2: PIN without diversification characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
x-enumDescriptions.ISO3: PIN diversified with the card account number and random characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
x-enumDescriptions.ISO4: PIN format used with AES encryption, conforming to the new ISO SC2 format.

pinBlockFormat.description:
Format of pin block
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ISO0 : PIN diversified with the card account number, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
ISO1 : PIN completed with random padding characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
ISO2 : PIN without diversification characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
ISO3 : PIN diversified with the card account number and random characters, conforming to the standard ISO 9564-2.
ISO4 : PIN format used with AES encryption, conforming to the new ISO SC2 format.
ISO5 : (NOT USED by JV) Alternative PIN format used with AES encryption, conforming to the new ISO SC2 format.
NPIN : No pin

encryptedPinBlock.description:
Encrypted Data
The data are encrypted using the key provided in the request

encryptedCardSecurityCode.description:
Field used to transmit the encrypted card security code (CVx)
The encryption is done using JSON Web Encryption standard.
Once decrypted it represents the card security code (CVx), which is a 3 numeric characters string.

closingDateTime.description:
Card agreement closing date and time
Date and time for the closure of the card agreement

On the closure request :
- If the date and time are present, the card agreement will be closed on that specific date and time
- Otherwise, the card agreement will be closed on the expiration of current active card (as a consequence stopping the automatic renewal process)

The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

code.description:
Card agreement option code
Code defining the option associated to the card agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
Example of values and label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
TRAVEL_OPTION : Travel option (subscription for reduced or canceled fees when using card in foreign countries)
CASH_LIMIT : Limit for cash that can be provided to a cardholder (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData
DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT : Limit for delegated withdrawal (used for premium cards) the amount must be provided in the optionData

data.description:
Data for the option
The values depend on the option code and are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
For example, the value must be present if optionCode is CASH_LIMIT or DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT

startDateTime.description:
Start date/time of the option
Start date/time of the option if the option has to be activated for a limited period of time
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

endDateTime.description:
End date/time of the option
End date/time of the option if the option has to be activated for a limited period of time
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

code.description:
Shipping condition code
Shipping condition code of the card contract/agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.

entityId.description:
Shipping condition entity Id
Identifier of the physical entity to be used for the shipping condition
For example, a branch to send the card

postalAddressId.description:
Shipping condition postal address identifier
The data of the postal address (this identifier and its corresponding details) will be obtained upon retrieval of the cardholder data.

postalAddress.description:
Temporary shipping condition postal address
The data are used to format the address on a letter as follows :
- Line 1 is set using the civility/firstName/lastName/company or entity Name data which are provided separately
- Line 2 is set using either the department (professionnal address) or the room (personal address)
- Line 3 is set using the floor
- Line 4 is set using the streetName
- Line 5 is set by concatenating the postBox and the townNameLocation
- Line 6 is set by concatenating the postCode and the townName
- Line 7 is set using the countryCode (country name determined from the ISO3166 numeric-3 code)
townName and countryCode are required.
At least one of (department, room, floor, streetName or townNameLocation) is required.
The department, room, floor, streetName, townNameLocation and townName are defined as 70 characters strings but, at this stage, the CMS service only used up to the first 38 characters.

startDateTime.description:
Start date and time of the temporary shipping condition
When a temporary shipping condition is set a start date and time is defined
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

endDateTime.description:
End date and time of the temporary shipping condition
When a temporary shipping condition is set an end date and time is defined
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

code.description:
Usage limit code
Code defining the context of use of the usage limit
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PDOM : Paiement domestique (Purchase DOMestic)
PINT : Paiement international (Purchase INTernational)​
PTOT : Paiement total (Purchase TOTal)
WGRD : Retrait domestique groupe (Withdrawal GRoup Domestic)
WNGD : Retrait domestique hors groupe et cash manuel domestique (Withdrawal Not Group Domestic)​
WGRI : Retrait international groupe (Withdrawal GRoup International)​
WNGI : Retrait international hors groupe et cash manuel international (Withdrawal Not Group International)
WTOT : Retrait total (Withdrawal TOTal)​

type.description:
Usage limit type
Code defining the type of the usage limit
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
OTHR : Other (reserved for future use)
STRD : Standard
TEMP : Temporary (reserved for future use)

valueType.description:
Type of value of the usage limit
Type defining whether the usage limit is an amount or a number
In the current definition, only amounts are supported.
The definition of the "number" type is reserved for future use
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
AMNT : Amount
NUBR : Number (RFU)

amount.description:
Amount of the usage limit
Amount and currency of the usage limit set at the product level in the configuration of the CMS (this is the default amount and currency of the limit)
This amount and currency are significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as AMNT
The curency is provided as a ISO 4217, Numeric-3 code.
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified by the currency code in compliance with ISO4217. The decimal separator is a dot.

personalizedAmount.description:
Personalized amount of the usage limit
Amount of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this amount overrides the default amount of the limit)
This amount is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as AMNT
This amount can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified in a currency where the unit of currency is implied by the context and compliant with ISO 4217. The decimal separator is a dot.

number.description:
Number of the usage limit
Number of the usage limit set at the product level in the configuration of the CMS (this is the default number of the limit)
This number is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)

personalizedNumber.description:
Personalized number of the usage limit
Number of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this number overrides the default number of the limit)
This number is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)
This number can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.

startDateTime.description:
Starting date and time of the temporary limit/usage
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

endDateTime.description:
Ending date and time of the temporary limit/usage
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

code.description:
Card usage code
Code defining the context of use of the usage current value
The usage current value code must be identical to the corresponding usage limit code

Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PDOM : Paiement domestique (Purchase DOMestic)
PINT : Paiement international (Purchase INTernational)​
PTOT : Paiement total (Purchase TOTal)
WGRD : Retrait domestique groupe (Withdrawal GRoup Domestic)
WNGD : Retrait domestique hors groupe et cash manuel domestique (Withdrawal Not Group Domestic)​
WGRI : Retrait international groupe (Withdrawal GRoup International)​
WNGI : Retrait international hors groupe et cash manuel international (Withdrawal Not Group International)
WTOT : Retrait total (Withdrawal TOTal)​

type.description:
Card limit type
Code defining the type of the usage current value and the usage current value limit
The usage current value limit type must be identical to the corresponding usage limit type
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
OTHR : Other (reserved for future use)
STRD : Standard
TEMP : Temporary (reserved for future use)

valueType.description:
valueType set at the product level
Type defining whether the usage current value and the usage current value limit are an amount or a number
In the current definition, only amounts are supported.
The definition of the "number" type is reserved for future use
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
AMNT : Amount
NUBR : Number (RFU)

limitAmount.description:
Current amount active for the limit
Current amount and currency active for the limit
This amount and currency are significant if the usageCurrentValueType is set as AMNT
This can be either the temporary personalized amount or the permanent personalized amount or the default amount
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified by the currency code in compliance with ISO4217. The decimal separator is a dot.
The curency is provided as a ISO 4217, Numeric-3 code.

currentAmount.description:
Amount of the current usage value
Current amount used on the limit
This amount is significant if the usageCurrentValueType is set as AMNT
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified in a currency where the unit of currency is implied by the context and compliant with ISO 4217. The decimal separator is a dot.

limitNumber.description:
Number of the usage limit
Current number active for the limit
This number is significant if the usageCurrentValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)
This number can be either the temporary or permanent personnalizedNumber or the default number

currentNumber.description:
Number of the current usage value
Current number used on the limit
This number is significant if the usageCurrentValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)

code.description:
Card usage restriction code
Code defining the usage restriction of the card
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
MOTO : Restriction on MOTO transactions
WDRL : Restriction on withdrawal transactions
PRCH : Restriction on purchase transactions
CTLS : Restriction on contactless transactions
GEOB : Restriction on geoblocking : the zone/region for which the geoblocking is applied must be provided in the usageRestrictionData

data.description:
Card usage restriction data
Data to be used for the usage restriction
The values depend on the usage restriction code.
The value must be used if usageRestrictionCode is GEOB to represent the zone/region on which the geoblocking is applied
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ZONE_A
ZONE_B
ZONE_C
ZONE_D
ZONE_E
ZONE_F
ZONE_G

startDateTime
Start date/time of the usage restriction if the usage restriction has to be activated for a limited period of time
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

endDateTime.description:
endDateTime
End date/time of the usage restriction if the usage restriction has to be activated for a limited period of time

VALD : Validated
ACIP : Activation in progress
ACTV : Activated
BLIP : Blocking in progress
BLKD : Blocked
DEIP : Deactivation in progress
DCTD : Deactivated

activationDateTime.description:
Activation date and time
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

alphaTestIndicator.description:
Alpha test indicator
Indicates if the card must be manufactured for alpha tests or not

cardholderPictureId.description:
Distribution identifier of the card design picture for the cardholder - may be an URL
Identifier of the card design picture for the cardholder provided by the bank during the subscription (for example, it may be an URL)

chainingMaskedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
Masked Primary Account Number of the chaining card
Displayable version of the PAN with masked caracters of the card to be used for chaining data from a previous card upon renewing the card (for example, retrieving the PIN code or personalized limits)

chainingSequenceNumber.description:
Card Sequence Number of the chaining card
Card sequence number of the card to be used for chaining data from a previous card upon renewing the card (for example, retrieving the PIN code or personalized limits)
Equivalent to DE23 in ISO8583 based protocols

chainingExpiryDate.description:
Expiry date of the chaining card
Expiry date of the card to be used for chaining data from a previous card upon renewing the card (for example, retrieving the PIN code or personalized limits)
Format (ISO8601) : YYYY-MM ; Example 2024-12
Equivalent to DE14 in ISO8583 based protocols

contactlessPresenceOnChipIndicator.description:
Contactless presence on the chip
Indicates whether the contacless feature is present on the chip or not

blockingDateTime.description:
Blocking date and time
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

blockingReasonCode.description:
Blocking reason code
Standard blocking reason code
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ABUS : Abusive
AUTB : Automatic blocking
CNTF : Counterfeit
FRAU : Fraudulent
HRFR : High risk fraudulent
LOST : Lost Card
LSRN : Lost or stolen during renewal
STLN : Stolen Card

blockingReference.description:
Blocking reference
Reference generated by the CMS when processing a card blocking request

contactlessActivationChoiceCode.description:
Contactless activation choice code
Code to activate or not the contacless function of the card depending on the cardholder choice at the subscription
Possible values are 'true' or 'false'

contactlessIndicator.description:
Contactless indicator
Indicates whether the contactless feature is activated or not on the card

lockingIndicator.description:
Card locking indicator
Indicates if the card is locked or not

lockingReasonCode.description:
Locking reason code
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ABUS : Abusive
CLRQ : Cardholder request
CNTF : Counterfeit
FRAU : Fraudulent
LOST : Lost Card (or Card Temporary Lost)
OTHR : Other

urgentManufacturingIndicator.description:
Urgent manufacturing indicator
Indicator to request an urgent manufacturing of the card
By default no urgent manufacturing is requested

designCode.description:
Card design code defined by the distribution
Code to define the design selected by the cardholder during the subscription and to be used to print the card

dynamicCardSecurityCodeIndicator.description:
Dynamic card security code indicator
Indicates whether the card security code is dynamic or not

embossedComplementaryData.description:
Complementary embossed zone label
Complementary line for the embossed zone to be printed on the card

embossedZone.description:
Card embossed zone label
Label to use for the embossed zone printed on the card in case the default format needs to be overridden

manufacturingReasonCode.description:
Manufacturing reason code
Code indicating the reason of the manufacturing of the card
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
INIT : Initialisation
SUBS : Subscription
REQD : Requested
AUTO : Automatical
RNWL : Renewal
EXTL : External
EMRP : Emergency_pan
TCLS : To_close
CLOS : Closure

Anticipated date and time for the CMS to send the card creation request to the manufacturer
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

manufacturingRequestRealDateTime.description:
Real manufacturing date and time
Real date and time when the CMS sent the card creation request to the manufacturer
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

manufacturingStatus.description:
Manufacturing status
Status representing the progress of the card manufacturing process
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
NDCL : Not_declared
DEPE : Declar_pending
DEDO : Declar_done
ACCP : Accepted
RFSD : Refused
MAND : Manufactured
NMAN : Not_manufactured
CLOS : Close

productCode.description:
Product Code
Product code of the card contract/agreement
The values to be used are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.

replacementCode.description:
Card replacement Code
Code indicating whether the card is a standard (regular) card or a replacement (emergency) card
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
STND : Standard
REPL : Replacement/Emergency card

replacementMaskedPrimaryAccountNumber.description:
Masked Primary Account Number of the replacement card
If the card product supports the replacement card feature (as indicated by replacementCardIndicator in the card agreement parameters), the PAN of the replacement card which can be displayed with masked caracters

remanufacturingReasonCode.description:
Remanufacturing reason code
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
BLCD : Blocked card
BRCD : Broken card
EACH : Embossed area change
LOCH : Locked chip
LSCD : Lost card
MARR : Manufacturing error
RNWL : Renewal
VICH : Visual change

surveillanceReasonCode.description:
Card agreement surveillance reason code
Reason code for the card agreement to be placed under surveillance
Example : NO_PROVISION
Complementary values can be defined in future version

lockingReasonCode.description:
Locking reason code
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ABUS : Abusive
CLRQ : Cardholder request
CNTF : Counterfeit
FRAU : Fraudulent
LOST : Lost Card (or Card Temporary Lost)
OTHR : Other

code.description:
Usage limit code
Code defining the context of use of the usage limit
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
PDOM : Paiement domestique (Purchase DOMestic)
PINT : Paiement international (Purchase INTernational)​
PTOT : Paiement total (Purchase TOTal)
WGRD : Retrait domestique groupe (Withdrawal GRoup Domestic)
WNGD : Retrait domestique hors groupe et cash manuel domestique (Withdrawal Not Group Domestic)​
WGRI : Retrait international groupe (Withdrawal GRoup International)​
WNGI : Retrait international hors groupe et cash manuel international (Withdrawal Not Group International)
WTOT : Retrait total (Withdrawal TOTal)​

valueType.description:
Type of value of the usage limit
Type defining whether the usage limit is an amount or a number
In the current definition, only amounts are supported.
The definition of the "number" type is reserved for future use
Value : Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------
AMNT : Amount
NUBR : Number (RFU)

personalizedAmount.description:
Personalized amount of the usage limit
Amount of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this amount overrides the default amount of the limit)
This amount is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as AMNT
This amount can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.
The amount is provided as a number of monetary units specified in a currency where the unit of currency is implied by the context and compliant with ISO 4217. The decimal separator is a dot.

personalizedNumber.description:
Personalized number of the usage limit
Number of the usage limit provided by the bank during the subscription or modified after the subscription (this number overrides the default number of the limit)
This number is significant if the usageLimitValueType is set as NUBR (RFU)
This number can be temporary if a start and end date/time are defined or permanent if no date/time are defined.

startDateTime.description:
Starting date and time of the temporary limit/usage
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

endDateTime.description:
Ending date and time of the temporary limit/usage
The date and time are specified in the following form (ISO8601 datetime format)

usageRestrictionData.description:
Card usage restriction data
Data to be used for the usage restriction
The values depend on the usage restriction code.
The value must be used if usageRestrictionCode is GEOB to represent the zone/region on which the geoblocking is applied
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ZONE_A
ZONE_B
ZONE_C
ZONE_D
ZONE_E
ZONE_F
ZONE_G

Card usage restriction data
Data to be used for the usage restriction
The values depend on the usage restriction code.
The value must be used if usageRestrictionCode is GEOB to represent the zone/region on which the geoblocking is applied
Value
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ZONE_A
ZONE_B
ZONE_C
ZONE_D
ZONE_E
ZONE_F
ZONE_G

Data for the option
The values depend on the option code and are defined in the parameters associated to the product catalog.
For example, the value must be present if optionCode is CASH_LIMIT or DELEGATED_WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT

This data allows to send discretionnary data from the bank to the card manufacturer or, in return, from the card manufacturer to the bank.
The CMS does not perform any action on this data.
